channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: The Cognitive Divergence: AI Context Windows, Human Attention Decline, and the Delegation Feedback Loop
item.description: arXiv:2603.26707v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper documents and theorises a self-reinforcing dynamic between two measurable trends: the exponential expansion of large language model (LLM) context windows and the secular contraction of human sustained-attention capacity. We term the resulti...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, context, attention, GPT, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Arithmetic OOD Failure Unfolds in Stages in Minimal GPTs
item.description: arXiv:2603.26828v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Arithmetic benchmarks are often reduced to a single held-out score, but that score can conflate qualitatively different failures. We study a controlled minimal GPT trained on exhaustive 2-digit addition, where all local digit transitions are already p...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, GPT, study, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Last Fingerprint: How Markdown Training Shapes LLM Prose
item.description: arXiv:2603.27006v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models produce em dashes at varying rates, and the observation that some models "overuse" them has become one of the most widely discussed markers of AI-generated text. Yet no mechanistic account of this pattern exists, and the parallel...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, GPT, large language model, fine-tuning, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Bitboard version of Tetris AI
item.description: arXiv:2603.26765v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The efficiency of game engines and policy optimization algorithms is crucial for training reinforcement learning (RL) agents in complex sequential decision-making tasks, such as Tetris. Existing Tetris implementations suffer from low simulation speeds...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, RAG, research, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Concerning Uncertainty -- A Systematic Survey of Uncertainty-Aware XAI
item.description: arXiv:2603.26838v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper surveys uncertainty-aware explainable artificial intelligence (UAXAI), examining how uncertainty is incorporated into explanatory pipelines and how such methods are evaluated. Across the literature, three recurring approaches to uncertainty...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, attention, paper, RAG, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Compliance-Aware Predictive Process Monitoring: A Neuro-Symbolic Approach
item.description: arXiv:2603.26948v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Existing approaches for predictive process monitoring are sub-symbolic, meaning that they learn correlations between descriptive features and a target feature fully based on data, e.g., predicting the surgical needs of a patient based on historical ev...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, knowledge base, paper, RAG, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Transparency as Architecture: Structural Compliance Gaps in EU AI Act Article 50 II
item.description: arXiv:2603.26983v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Art. 50 II of the EU Artificial Intelligence Act mandates dual transparency for AI-generated content: outputs must be labeled in both human-understandable and machine-readable form for automated verification. This requirement, entering into force in A...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, alignment, RAG, research, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: When Verification Hurts: Asymmetric Effects of Multi-Agent Feedback in Logic Proof Tutoring
item.description: arXiv:2603.27076v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for automated tutoring, but their reliability in structured symbolic domains remains unclear. We study step-level feedback for propositional logic proofs, which require precise symbolic reasoning alig...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, context, large language model, study, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Boundary-aware Prototype-driven Adversarial Alignment for Cross-Corpus EEG Emotion Recognition
item.description: arXiv:2603.26713v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG)-based emotion recognition suffers from severe performance degradation when models are transferred across heterogeneous datasets due to physiological variability, experimental paradigm differences, and device inconsistencie...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, alignment, RAG, experiment, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Step Toward Federated Pretraining of Multimodal Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.26786v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rapid evolution of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) is bottlenecked by the saturation of high-quality public data, while vast amounts of diverse multimodal data remain inaccessible in privacy-sensitive silos. Federated Learning (FL) offers...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, multimodal, cross-modal, alignment, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Robust Batch-Level Query Routing for Large Language Models under Cost and Capacity Constraints
item.description: arXiv:2603.26796v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study the problem of routing queries to large language models (LLMs) under cost, GPU resources, and concurrency constraints. Prior per-query routing methods often fail to control batch-level cost, especially under non-uniform or adversarial batchin...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, large language model, study, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: GeoBlock: Inferring Block Granularity from Dependency Geometry in Diffusion Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.26675v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Block diffusion enables efficient parallel refinement in diffusion language models, but its decoding behavior depends critically on block size. Existing block-sizing strategies rely on fixed rules or heuristic signals and do not account for the depend...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, attention, experiment, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A large corpus of lucid and non-lucid dream reports
item.description: arXiv:2603.26992v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: All varieties of dreaming remain a mystery. Lucid dreams in particular, or those characterized by awareness of the dream, are notoriously difficult to study. Their scarce prevalence and resistance to deliberate induction make it difficult to obtain a ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, ICL, study, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Mitigating Forgetting in Continual Learning with Selective Gradient Projection
item.description: arXiv:2603.26671v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As neural networks are increasingly deployed in dynamic environments, they face the challenge of catastrophic forgetting, the tendency to overwrite previously learned knowledge when adapting to new tasks, resulting in severe performance degradation on...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, experiment, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DSO: Dual-Scale Neural Operators for Stable Long-term Fluid Dynamics Forecasting
item.description: arXiv:2603.26800v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-term fluid dynamics forecasting is a critically important problem in science and engineering. While neural operators have emerged as a promising paradigm for modeling systems governed by partial differential equations (PDEs), they often struggle ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
